Title: Insights into stereoselective ring formation in canonical strigolactone: Identification of a dirigent domain-containing enzyme catalyzing orobanchol synthesis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone inhibiting shoot branching/tillering; rhizosphere signal promoting arbuscular mycorrhizal fungal hyphal branching; germination stimulant for root parasitic weeds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Sequentially oxidizes the C18 position of carlactonoic acid to 18-hydroxycarlactonoic acid and then to 18-oxocarlactonoic acid
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced under phosphate deficiency in roots (inferred for strigolactone biosynthesis genes)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate deficiency; mildly acidic conditions promote nonenzymatic cyclization of 18-oxo-CLA
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes orobanchol biosynthesis by generating the immediate precursor 18-oxo-CLA; without SRF the product can spontaneously cyclize into nonstereoselective orobanchol diastereomers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified recombinant enzyme assay in E. coli; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; methyl-ester derivatization matching authentic 18-oxocarlactonoate; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with SlCPR2
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream step supplying the cyclization substrate; rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Previously published tomato phosphate-response transcriptome used for candidate mining (no accession stated here for tomato); cowpea transcriptome DDBJ SRA: DRA008222 used for coexpression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering canonical strigolactone composition when combined with SRF to control stereoselective orobanchol production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alone can lead to spontaneous formation of mixed orobanchol diastereomers if SRF activity is absent
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper revises the exact function previously attributed to SlCYP722C, showing it does not directly form orobanchol enzymatically but instead forms 18-oxo-CLA, which then requires SRF for stereoselective cyclization. Recognizes natural (11R)-CLA but not (11S)-CLA.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Recombinant SlCYP722C converted CLA to 18-OH-CLA and 18-oxo-CLA. 2. Methyl-ester derivatization and LC-MS/MS identified the CLA+14 product as 18-oxocarlactonoic acid. 3. SlCYP722C accepts (11R)-CLA but not (11S)-CLA. 4. In transient expression assays, SlCYP722C produced 18-oxo-CLA and, without SlSRF, nonstereoselective orobanchol diastereomers.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone inhibiting shoot branching/tillering; rhizosphere signal promoting arbuscular mycorrhizal fungal hyphal branching; germination stimulant for root parasitic weeds
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal step for stereoselective orobanchol formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes stereoselective BC-ring closure of 18-oxocarlactonoic acid to form orobanchol
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dirigent family, DIR-f subfamily, dirigent domain-containing protein (Pfam PF03018)
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated via N-terminal signal peptide/transmembrane region
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expected to be induced by phosphate deficiency; candidate selected from phosphate deficiency-upregulated tomato root genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate deficiency in roots; acidic conditions otherwise cause spontaneous nonenzymatic cyclization of substrate
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically promotes stereoselective formation of orobanchol and prevents nonenzymatic generation of mixed diastereomers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified recombinant enzyme assay; transient coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in tomato; metabolite profiling of root exudates; subcellular localization; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant and key stereochemical control step
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SlCYP722C; possible metabolon with SlCYP722C and SlCYP712G1 (proposed, not directly proven)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Tomato phosphate-response transcriptome from Wang et al. used for candidate selection; cowpea transcriptome DDBJ SRA: DRA008222 used in coexpression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to tailor strigolactone stereochemistry, alter rhizosphere signaling, and potentially reduce germination of parasitic weeds by modifying the orobanchol/ent-2′-epi-orobanchol ratio
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout impairs downstream orobanchol metabolism, with loss of solanacol and near absence of didehydro-orobanchol isomers, potentially altering beneficial rhizosphere functions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as Solyc01g059900. Catalytic residues supported by mutagenesis and modeling include D37, D70, Y90, D124, and R131; D37 acts as proton donor. DIR-f biochemical function was previously unknown.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Solyc01g059900/SlSRF was identified from phosphate-responsive and coexpression analyses as a dirigent-domain candidate linked to orobanchol biosynthesis. 2. Recombinant SlSRF converted 18-oxo-CLA stereoselectively to orobanchol. 3. Coexpression with SlCYP722C in N. benthamiana enabled stereoselective production of orobanchol from CLA. 4. CRISPR knockout lines accumulated both orobanchol and ent-2′-epi-orobanchol and lost downstream metabolites, proving SlSRF is required in planta for stereoselective orobanchol biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant hormone inhibiting shoot branching/tillering; rhizosphere signal promoting arbuscular mycorrhizal fungal hyphal branching; germination stimulant for root parasitic weeds
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Terminal step for stereoselective orobanchol formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes stereoselective BC-ring closure of 18-oxocarlactonoic acid to form orobanchol
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with known orobanchol biosynthesis genes in a cowpea transcriptome module
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate deficiency context mentioned for strigolactone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a cowpea SRF candidate supporting stereoselective orobanchol production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cowpea candidate biochemical assay with recombinant protein and 18-oxo-CLA substrate; coexpression network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant and stereochemical control step
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Loss of VuSRF function is proposed to permit ent-2′-epi-orobanchol production and may help confer resistance to Striga gesnerioides by reducing germination
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not experimentally determined in planta in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally supported cowpea SRF candidate corresponds to Vigun03g413900; Vigun03g413800 was tested but not shown to act as SRF.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two cowpea homologs of SlSRF were identified by coexpression analysis. 2. Biochemical assays showed Vigun03g413900, designated VuSRF, catalyzes conversion of 18-oxo-CLA to orobanchol. 3. The paper proposes VuSRF as a breeding target for parasite-resistance-oriented SL tailoring.
